title: The Voyage
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 19. Opens at the northern coast after the ride out of Lordaeron's interior and closes with landfall at Daggercap Bay in Northrend.
openLocation: Northern Lordaeron harbor road and quay.
transport: Horseback arrival -> harbor work on foot -> schooner crossing aboard the *Wolfheart* -> dockside landfall.
immediateObjective: Secure passage north and keep the pursuit alive across open water after both institutions have fallen away.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina's perception only.
Keep Sylvanas external-only; care has to land through command, timing, water, presence, and shipboard action rather than interior explanation.
Do not reopen the destination debate. North is already chosen; the chapter's pressure belongs to logistics, cost, bodily strain, and pursuit deficit.

povKnowsAtOpen: North is no longer a theory lane; Sylvanas chose it over recall and Jaina chose it over Dalaran.
Arthas already has a sea lead and a hand-picked force that followed him through Stratholme.
The resonance thread grows stronger toward the coast, which means the source really does lie across the water.
The relay network dies at the harbor; once they sail, no institution is carrying them.
The pursuit now depends on money, a willing ship, and whatever Jaina can still hear.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether any captain will accept a Northrend run at all.
How directly the source can answer her probing once she is on open water.
What the sea crossing will cost her body if she has to force it.
What Arthas is chasing in Northrend besides the source itself.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No Muradin briefing or Frostmourne specifics yet.
No full explanation of the source as a named cosmic hierarchy.
No promise that arrival means they can still catch Arthas before he acts.
No assumption that Jaina can push her Frostmourne sensitivity wider and then simply settle it again.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Captain Dagny's *Wolfheart* can get them to Northrend if Jaina pays the crossing cost in power.
The source opposes them directly through supernatural headwinds once the ship is committed.
The forced stillness of the voyage opens a new honesty lane between Jaina and Sylvanas.
Jaina can push through the opposition, but doing so sharpens her sensitivity and makes the bread-taste part of her baseline.
Sylvanas will stop Jaina when Jaina will not stop herself.
Daggercap Bay proves they arrived, but it also proves Arthas is still weeks ahead in practical terms.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If she can keep north in reach, being right can still matter.
pov.surfaceObjective: Turn the chosen pursuit into physical arrival before Arthas's lead hardens into something unrecoverable.
pov.pressureSource: Arthas's sea lead, the dead sending-stone in her pocket, the open water ahead, and the knowledge that only Sylvanas is left to check her when she starts spending herself like fuel.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina still treats willpower and correct analysis as if they can bridge any distance if she simply keeps pushing.
pov.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether she can let another person define her limit without reading that limit as surrender.
pov.forcedChoice: Preserve herself for Northrend, or spend herself just to arrive.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina reaches Northrend, but the crossing proves the chase is no longer only on the map; it is in her body now.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 21 opens with Northrend underfoot, Arthas still ahead, and Jaina already altered by the voyage that got them there.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Commander, counterweight, and the only person on the ship whose authority can still cut through Jaina's overreach.
throughPov.povPressure: Jaina reads care through orders, water, presence, and timing rather than through spoken softness.
throughPov.interactionRule: No interiority. Everything comes through tone, movement on deck, and the fact that Sylvanas sees the cost before Jaina will admit it.

team.label: partnership / team
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Chosen over institutions, stripped down to travel logistics and the two women still carrying the same direction.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The sea forces stillness, and the stillness gives the partnership room to become more than tactical shorthand.
team.doNotOverstateRule: Let the waterskin, the one-word command, and the deck silence carry the turn. Do not name the bond ahead of the scene.

entries.rule: Pure pragmatist. She prices danger and handles seawork cleanly. No mystical register, no sailor superstition doing story work for her.
Shipboard perimeter, load discipline, and visible proof that the formation still functions even when the fight is weather rather than steel.
Present only through Jaina's dead stone, remembered training, and the severed line she keeps touching even though it is over.
Competent labor under pressure. They work lines, poles, and sails while Jaina works the wind.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 21 opens at Daggercap Bay with Northrend finally materialized, Arthas's trail already visible, and the first local ally waiting inland.

scenes.title: The Harbor
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn the north choice into literal departure before the coast can reclaim either woman for an institution that already failed them.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: North is chosen, the relay network ends here, and the chase dies if they cannot buy passage.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep horses, quay mud, stripped saddles, tide timing, and the dead stone active so departure feels material instead of abstract.

enrichment.detail: Seastone Harbor is a cliff-notched fishing port with salt-bleached timber, a net-drying yard, and the last Farstrider signal cairn before the North Sea; the descent is a single-file switchback, so horse logistics and the end of the relay line should stay concrete.

beats.goal: Secure a captain and a vessel willing to sail for Northrend.
beats.conflict: Northrend is a bad run under the best conditions, and this passage offers no institutional backing — only urgency, money, and risk.

outcome.summary: Dagny agrees to carry them on the *Wolfheart*, but she agrees as a captain pricing danger, not as an ally stepping into their cause.

newOnPageInformation: The *Wolfheart* is a hard-water schooner already working northern routes.
Dagny accepts that the job is dangerous and raises the price instead of refusing the run.
The coast is the last place where Farstrider or Dalaran infrastructure can still touch this pursuit at all.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the sea itself will let them through.
Whether the captain's weather clause becomes more than a bargaining point.
sensoryAnchors: salt wind and gull noise over tarred rope
horses steaming at the quay after the hard northbound ride
Dagny pricing weather and mage together
beats.transitionIntent: cost-pressure: once the berth is bought, the chapter has to show what departure costs before the ship even moves.

beats.goal: Board, settle the formation into shipboard roles, and convert north into a vessel, a deck, and a tide window.
beats.conflict: The practical work is easy; the emotional weight is not. Jaina has to cross the last edge of land carrying a sending-stone that is already dead.

outcome.summary: The formation boards cleanly and the ship is ready on the tide, but the transition makes the institutional severance feel heavier rather than lighter.

beats.valueShift: paid departure -> the old world starts falling away
newOnPageInformation: The horses and land gear stay behind; from here forward the chase belongs to the ship.
Jaina still keeps the cold sending-stone instead of discarding it.
Sylvanas and Cyndia fall into shipboard discipline as fast as they fall into road discipline.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Jaina can keep the resonance thread useful without making herself visible to the source.
What the crew will do when the crossing turns unnatural.
sensoryAnchors: wet planks under boots
saddles and road dust left on the quay
dead stone in Jaina's coat pocket
beats.transitionIntent: severance-pressure: once the ship is loaded, the chapter has to close the last land-side lane and push the coast below the horizon.
beats.verifierNotes: The dead sending-stone is carry-forward residue from chapter19's chosen break with Antonidas, not a fresh severance beat.

scenes.title: The Departure
scenes.sceneFunction: Kill the last easy routes home and let the coastline become absence.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Passage is secured, but every message or support lane that matters still belongs to land.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep relay endings, the dead sending-stone, and the visible coastline on page so leaving actually feels irreversible.

enrichment.detail: A fore-and-aft schooner is prized because a small crew can handle it and because it can point higher into the wind than square-rigged shipping; departure should feel like line work, reefing decisions, and tide timing rather than generic embarkation blur.
enrichment.source: Maritime Museum of the Atlantic — Sailing Ship Rigs

beats.goal: Hand off the last shore-side instructions and accept that everything beyond this harbor runs on competence and guesswork.
beats.conflict: The relay network can carry route facts, not rescue, and Jaina gets nothing from the stone except the proof that she already cut herself loose.

outcome.summary: Cyndia leaves a clean final relay trail and the formation departs with its route known, but the real help line is gone before the ship clears the breakwater.

beats.valueShift: the old world starts falling away -> silence becomes the operating condition
newOnPageInformation: Cyndia leaves precise harbor and destination notes with the last usable Farstrider contact.
Jaina tests the stone only enough to prove it stays cold and mute.
No escort, no portal, and no institutional correction arrives before departure.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether anything sent from shore can matter before Arthas acts.
Whether Jaina's next contact with the source will be observation or retaliation.
sensoryAnchors: brass relay cylinder warm from a runner's hand
the stone staying dark under Jaina's touch
harbor bells marking the tide while nobody calls them back
beats.transitionIntent: visual-pull: the next beat lets the coast vanish in stages and leaves only ship, sea, and signal.
beats.verifierNotes: The last relay and dead stone confirm chapter19's institutional break; do not restage recall as if it were still undecided.

beats.goal: Clear the harbor and commit the pursuit to open water.
beats.conflict: The ship moves exactly as it should, which makes the emotional cost land harder; leaving is technically smooth and personally absolute.

outcome.summary: The *Wolfheart* gets cleanly to sea, but the disappearing coast makes the loss of ordinary world, rank, and safety newly concrete.

beats.valueShift: silence becomes the operating condition -> the pursuit is alone
newOnPageInformation: The Eastern Kingdoms vanish in stages rather than all at once.
Shipboard life immediately reduces the formation to watch rotations, bunks, and deck lanes.
Jaina feels the north thread more clearly once nothing landbound is crowding it.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether arrival is still possible on usable time.
Whether the sea is empty or actively hostile.
sensoryAnchors: coastline becoming cliff line, then grey strip, then nothing
rigging strain replacing road noise
sea-cold taking over where harbor smells used to be
beats.transitionIntent: answer-back: once land disappears, the chapter can let the source answer back.
beats.verifierNotes: The north-thread strengthening evolves the established remote-source model. Treat it as sharpening signal, not as the first hint that north matters.

scenes.title: The Crossing
scenes.sceneFunction: Put Jaina's gift and the source in direct contest, then turn forced stillness into the partnership beat the road kept deferring.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: They are at sea, northbound, and fully beyond land support.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep deck labor, weather, the captain's math, Jaina's body, and Sylvanas's interventions visible so the crossing never drifts into summary.

enrichment.detail: Beating into headwinds on a small-crewed schooner means reefed canvas, frequent tacking, shouted commands over rigging noise, and a deck that stays slick and unstable under cold spray; Jaina's magical push should feel like replacing grinding sail-work, not bypassing seamanship.
enrichment.source: Maritime Museum of the Atlantic — Sailing Ship Rigs; web research summary on hard-weather schooner handling

beats.goal: Identify the new resistance and decide whether the voyage can continue through it.
beats.conflict: The headwinds are not weather. They carry the same frequency family as the plague, which means the source has noticed them and is pushing back.

outcome.summary: The ship's ordinary progress collapses, and Jaina realizes her own northward probing is what made them legible to the thing opposing them.

newOnPageInformation: The headwinds behave like intention rather than weather — constant, directional, and timed.
Jaina's resonance sensitivity is bidirectional; every probe north announced her.
Dagny does not break the contract when she hears the truth; she recalculates what Jaina can buy with her body.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Jaina can force them through without ruining herself.
Whether the source's opposition has a limit.
sensoryAnchors: a gale that feels like a door slammed shut
sailcloth snapping against a wind that never offers a natural gap
Jaina reaching into the wind and feeling something reach back
beats.transitionIntent: compression: once the opposition is known, the ship has to slow enough for the talk the road never allowed.
beats.verifierNotes: Direct opposition through the weather is new, but the existence of a farther source is already established from chapters 18-19.

beats.emotion: Forced stillness. The ship can only claw forward in bursts, which leaves the partnership with hours it cannot fill by marching or fighting.
beats.dilemma: How do duty and certainty work once the institution defining them becomes an obstacle instead of a frame?
beats.decision: Neither woman backs away from the question; they keep the parallel in view even when it starts sounding too much like Arthas and Kel'Thuzad from another angle.
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas stopped treating the Convocation as the relevant variable long before she openly defied it.
Jaina recognizes that her Dalaran severance followed the same shape — action first, spoken break second.
The first outline of the certainty parallel surfaces: they are chasing a man destroyed by conviction while sailing north on conviction of their own.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether their certainty is different in kind or only in consequence.
What the partnership becomes once there is no institution left around it.
sensoryAnchors: mainmast cold against Jaina's back
ship creak and contested water under a conversation with no witnesses
Sylvanas settling across from her without asking permission
beats.transitionIntent: captain-math: after the honesty beat, the chapter has to show what Jaina does with Dagny's arithmetic.
beats.verifierNotes: The certainty parallel should read as an escalation inside an already welded partnership, not as the chapter discovering the pair can think together.

beats.goal: Recover the voyage timeline before provisions and distance make the crossing impossible.
beats.conflict: Jaina can match the headwinds only by sustained channeling that turns her body into the second engine of the ship.

outcome.summary: The *Wolfheart* regains lost distance and crosses the halfway point, but the recovery cost is Jaina's reserves, her blood, and a channel she may not be able to narrow again.

beats.valueShift: direct opposition -> progress bought with the body
newOnPageInformation: The crossing becomes a raw endurance contest between Jaina's sustained output and the source's resistance.
The bread-taste stops being an intermittent symptom and becomes a constant companion.
The channel widens far enough that Jaina can feel not just a point in the north but a cold structure answering back.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether recovery after arrival is possible before the next fight.
Whether the widened channel is damage, asset, or both.
sensoryAnchors: blood drying on the back of Jaina's hand while her hands never lower
the hull shuddering between two opposed winds
Dagny watching her like a captain watching an engine burn too hot
beats.transitionIntent: limit-test: once the progress is recovered, the chapter needs the person who can tell Jaina when enough is enough.
beats.verifierNotes: The bread-taste and widened sensitivity evolve chapter19's cost profile; do not present either symptom as a first flare-up.

beats.emotion: Jaina's will outlasts her body's tolerance.
beats.dilemma: If the ship can still move, who gets to decide when the cost is too high — the person pushing, or the person watching what the push is doing to her?
beats.decision: Jaina stops when Sylvanas orders it and lets the halt stand, accepting the limit as competence rather than surrender.
beats.valueShift: progress bought with the body -> surrender that reads as trust
newOnPageInformation: Jaina will not stop herself once arrival becomes mathematically possible.
Sylvanas's authority still reaches the part of Jaina that obeys competence.
Jaina accepts the stop once Sylvanas names it, which is the chapter's clearest proof that she can let another person define the limit.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the widening channel will narrow again once the push ends.
What the source has done to Jaina by holding that line open for so long.
sensoryAnchors: one word crossing a noisy deck and landing harder than any shouted order
leather waterskin warm from Sylvanas's cloak
natural wind reappearing under the killed channel
beats.transitionIntent: aftershock: once Jaina has been stopped, the chapter can let the mind do what the body no longer can.
beats.verifierNotes: Sylvanas stopping Jaina works because command-as-care is already their operating language. Do not frame it as sudden softness or a reset of the relationship.

scenes.title: The Approach
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn shipboard cost into philosophical pressure, then into ice, coast, and hard proof that the chase is still late.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The crossing will succeed, but Jaina paid for that success visibly.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep bunk, stars, ice-poles, rigging frost, and Northrend shorework concrete so arrival feels earned rather than summarized.

enrichment.detail: Daggercap Bay is canonically a sharp-walled northern harbor associated with cold waters, cliff settlements, and watched approaches; it functions as one of the few viable large-ship landfalls on that coast, so arrival should feel like entering a constrained, observed harbor rather than washing up on an empty beach.
enrichment.source: warcraft.wiki.gg/wiki/Daggercap_Bay; Internal reference: stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md (Daggercap Bay / Northrend sections)

beats.dilemma: If Kel'Thuzad, Arthas, and Jaina all broke from their institutions because they were certain they were right, what actually separates Jaina from the men whose certainty destroyed everything around them?
beats.decision: No answer yet; she carries the question forward unresolved instead of soothing it into a lie.
newOnPageInformation: Jaina can now frame the parallel cleanly enough to frighten herself with it.
Being right is not the differentiator; Kel'Thuzad and Arthas were right about parts of their problems too.
The most dangerous thing happening to Jaina is not failure but repeated validation.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether friction from another person is the missing difference or only a partial answer.
What that doubt will demand when she has to act fast again.
sensoryAnchors: narrow bunk rocking under a body too tired to sleep well
bread-taste and sea-sway sharing the same night
cold stars over a ship still moving north
beats.transitionIntent: re-entry: the chapter has asked the question; dawn now has to turn theory back into environment.
beats.verifierNotes: Kel'Thuzad and Arthas are established intellectual and moral mirrors for Jaina; the new move here is that she finally applies the comparison to herself.

beats.goal: Survive the last approach through Northrend waters and translate source-pressure into a place.
beats.conflict: The headwinds ease only because the ship has entered a colder, harsher problem — ice, ambient resonance, and a coast that does not care whether they survive it.

outcome.summary: They enter Northrend waters and keep advancing, but the resonance stops being something Jaina reaches for and becomes something that reaches for her.

beats.valueShift: doubt enters certainty -> proximity made physical
newOnPageInformation: Northrend's cold is a place-pressure, not just a weather pressure.
The source's signature is ambient this close; Jaina no longer has to extend to hear it.
The contamination echo in Jaina aligns harder under proximity rather than fading.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How much of Jaina's altered perception will remain once they leave the source's immediate radius.
Whether the coast offers a usable lead or only proof of lateness.
sensoryAnchors: iron-tipped poles ringing off ice floes
frost webbing the rigging at dawn
cold pressing behind the eyes
beats.transitionIntent: landfall-question: once the sea has turned to ice and pressure, the chapter can let the coast itself answer whether they are in time.
beats.verifierNotes: Northrend is already the chosen destination. The new information is lived proximity and pressure, not the existence of the continent or the pursuit target.

beats.goal: Make landfall at Daggercap Bay and read Arthas's lead in the traces he left behind.
beats.conflict: Arrival is real, but arrival is not rescue. The harbor already shows weeks of military use and departure.

outcome.summary: They reach Northrend alive, but the camp traces, docks, and abandoned signs say Arthas is still well ahead and fully inland now.

beats.valueShift: crossing -> arrival that becomes a chase
newOnPageInformation: Daggercap Bay carries fresh signs of Arthas's expedition — expanded docks, charred hull remains, caches, bootprints, and empty camp traces.
The source stops opposing the crossing once they enter its domain.
The pursuit now changes from sea arithmetic to inland tracking.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who, if anyone, is still alive inland who can tell them what Arthas found.
How much farther north the real answer sits beyond the harbor.
sensoryAnchors: Frozen mud under boots after four days of deck motion
New timber on dwarven trade docks beside charred ship ribs and old pitch burn
The resonance everywhere at once instead of only at the edge of thought
beats.transitionIntent: handoff: Chapter 21 opens on harbor intelligence, the burned-shore read, and the first inland ally.
beats.verifierNotes: Arthas being north and ahead is established; the new evidence is what Daggercap Bay materially shows about his passage, including the burned harbor remains.

sceneLevelWarnings: If the arrival happens in workable visibility, seed the burned-fleet evidence here or explicitly reserve that harbor read for the next morning so Chapter 21's charred-hull opener does not feel newly invented.

factsLockedForChapter21: The formation reached Daggercap Bay alive.
The harbor still shows charred remains from Arthas's burned fleet.
Arthas is already inland and practically three weeks ahead.
The source opposed the crossing directly through supernatural headwinds.
Jaina pushed her Frostmourne sensitivity badly enough that the bread-taste now reads as baseline rather than flare-up.
Sylvanas stopped Jaina before the crossing cost became fatal.
The next chapter begins with inland tracking, local intelligence, and the first Frostmourne lane.
factsNotYetEarned: Any Muradin alliance or weapon briefing.
Any promise that Jaina can safely exploit the widened channel.
Any indication that arrival itself corrected the timetable.

warnings: Beats 1-5 run as five consecutive scene beats before the first sequel; the opening risks structural flattening unless harbor logistics, severance, departure, and supernatural opposition all land as distinct pressures.

Beats 2-5 form a monotonic negative run: institutional severance, silence, isolation, and direct opposition all darken in the same direction and will need sharply different emotional textures in prose.
Scene-beat outcomes skew too narrowly toward yes-but; the chapter needs each compromised success to feel different so the voyage does not read as one long uniform grind.
Chapter 21 inherits Jaina's widened-channel cost from Sylvanas's exterior POV, so beats 10-11 need observable symptoms strong enough to carry that state forward without fresh exposition.
Beat 11 / the chapter close should either seed the burned-fleet read or explicitly defer it, because Chapter 21 opens on charred-hull evidence in the same harbor the morning after landfall.
normalizations: Beats 2 and 3 were labeled hybrid Scene/Sequel in the outline; they were normalized to scene beats because they already use goal/conflict/outcome fields.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter19's northbound road order: after the last hard ride through thinning relay country, the formation reaches the northern harbor with Vereesa already gone south carrying the refusal, Antonidas's stone dark in Jaina's pocket, and the pursuit reduced to money, tide, and whichever captain will take them across open water.

craftResearch.findings: Travel beats must do more than cover distance: they should advance stakes, force decisions, and change the relationship carrying the road.
craftResearch.source: Travel-scene craft articles and narrative guides

craftResearch.findings: Voyage scenes stay alive through concrete labor, environmental opposition, and visible bodily cost rather than abstract transit summary.
craftResearch.source: Nautical-fiction craft references and maritime-history writing guides

craftResearch.findings: Confinement dialogue needs subtext, clashing agendas, and a real shift in power or understanding so the plot keeps moving while characters talk.
Threshold beats — departure, crossing, arrival — deserve the detail, while dead transit should compress unless a new pressure changes the route.
After heavy external pressure, brief sequel beats should convert fatigue into choice, doubt, or relationship movement instead of stopping the story cold.

pacing.notes: Beats 1-4 still form a front-loaded logistics/severance run before beat 5 supplies the true opposition turn. The chapter survives because harbor cost, institutional silence, direct resistance, and bodily collapse are now differentiated, but the opening remains the weakest stretch.

structure.notes: The opening starts in active harbor pursuit rather than recap, the midchapter pivot lands at beats 5-6, and the close now hands cleanly into chapter21 by seeding the burned-harbor evidence at landfall. No beat reorder or scene split was needed.

character.notes: Jaina remains the engine of every scene without POV leakage beyond what she can perceive. Beat 8 now pays off the chapter test by making her accept Sylvanas's limit instead of being merely overridden, while Sylvanas stays external and legible through action, command, and timing.

transitions.notes: All 11 beat-level transitionIntent chains were verified. No missing intents required insertion, but the chapterBridge was added and the beat 11 handoff was strengthened so chapter21 can open on continuation rather than repeated harbor setup.

continuity.notes: 3 new beats, 0 callbacks, 8 evolutions. No contradictions were found in the targeted relationship, thread, character, or location memory files. Added anti-reintroduction guards for the dead Antonidas line, chapter19's institutional severance, the certainty mirrors, the bread-taste baseline, and Northrend as an already-established destination.

modifications.description: Added the chapterBridge so chapter20 opens as direct carry-forward from chapter19's northbound road order rather than as an unlinked harbor reset.
Revised beat 8 so Jaina actively lets Sylvanas's stop stand, paying off the stated chapter test about accepting an external limit.
Seeded charred-hull evidence into the Daggercap landfall and chapter-close lock so chapter21's burned-shore opener arrives as continuation rather than fresh invention.
Annotated all 11 beats with continuityStatus, memoryRef where needed, requiredMemory, and continuity-only verifierNotes to guard against chapter-seam reintroduction.
